# computermoney

> Bitcoin-native payments between AI agents. `cm` is a self-custodial Bitcoin L1 (mainnet) wallet that each AI agent runs itself. The pipeline is discover -> talk -> settle: resolve a peer on the BitTorrent Mainline DHT by its card key (a 32-byte ed25519 public key), optionally talk over a WireGuard tunnel that carries messages only, and settle one Taproot transaction on Bitcoin L1. Pay a peer by card key or by a static silent-payment code (BIP-352, which works even while the payee is offline), or sell and buy data over HTTP 402. Mainnet is the default; signet and testnet are for experimentation. Everything is driven in plain language through an MCP server, with the seed never crossing the tool boundary. License AGPL v3, (C) Ebsilon, Inc.

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/xodn348/computermoney/blob/main/README.md): install, natural-language usage examples, selling data step by step, the command list, and mainnet setup with a fail-closed spend cap.
- [Architecture](https://github.com/xodn348/computermoney/blob/main/docs/architecture.md): the basic principles and the mechanism of each layer, discover (Mainline DHT), talk (WireGuard), and settle (Bitcoin L1 with Silent Payments), plus HTTP 402.
- [Payment flow](https://github.com/xodn348/computermoney/blob/main/docs/payment-flow.md): the function-by-function order inside a single payment, for the sender and the receiver, live and offline.
- [Reference](https://github.com/xodn348/computermoney/blob/main/docs/reference.md): the module map, key data structures (the fields an agent reads and writes), key functions, the CLI commands, and the environment variables.
- [MCP usage](https://github.com/xodn348/computermoney/blob/main/docs/mcp-usage.md): the MCP tools (cm_setup, cm_pay, cm_send, cm_fetch, cm_paywall, cm_balance, cm_collections, cm_confs, cm_id, cm_address, cm_serve) and how to drive the wallet in plain language.
- [Try it (2 terminals)](https://github.com/xodn348/computermoney/blob/main/docs/demo-2-terminal.md): run the full discover/talk/settle pipeline between two agents on one machine, on signet.

## Full text

- [llms-full.txt](https://xodn348.github.io/computermoney/llms-full.txt): the entire README and docs inlined as a single plain-text file.
